commit.message:
Move md_get_pdb_id to dnmd_pdb.h and reduce CI matrix (#70)

* Move md_get_pdb_id to dnmd_pdb.h and fix size query pattern

Move the md_get_pdb_id declaration from dnmd.h to dnmd_pdb.h where
it belongs alongside other Portable PDB APIs.

Fix the implementation to always write back the required buffer size
before checking if the caller's buffer is large enough, enabling
callers to query the needed size with a too-small buffer.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce CI matrix to sparse configuration

Replace the full 12-leg matrix (3 OS × 2 flavors × 2 vendored) with
a sparse 8-leg matrix to reduce CI costs while maintaining coverage:
- Ubuntu: Debug and Release, both vendored modes (4 legs)
- macOS: Debug vendored, Release non-vendored (2 legs)
- Windows: Release vendored, Debug non-vendored (2 legs)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update app/events/routes.py, store recent 50 events instead of infinite

Storing created_events in Flask’s default client-side session can overflow the signed cookie size if many lines are submitted, causing requests to fail. Consider limiting what’s stored (e.g., last N events) or persisting server-side instead of putting the full list into the session cookie.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Sync eng/common directory with azure-sdk-tools for PR 14461 (#2149)

* Add DirectoryFilterPattern parameter to regeneration pipeline

Add DirectoryFilterPattern parameter to archetype-typespec-emitter.yml
and New-RegenerateMatrix.ps1 to allow filtering the regeneration matrix
by package directory name pattern.

This enables azure-sdk-for-net's http-client-csharp-mgmt CI to only
regenerate Azure.ResourceManager* SDKs instead of all TypeSpec SDKs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Handle empty filter results with array wrapping and early error

Address review feedback: wrap filtered results in @() to ensure
reliable .Count, and fail fast with a clear error when no directories
match the filter pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Support comma-separated filter patterns

Update DirectoryFilterPattern to split on commas and match any pattern,
so callers can filter to multiple SDK prefixes (e.g.,
'Azure.ResourceManager*,Azure.Provisioning*').

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Wei Hu <live1206@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add md_get_pdb_id API for reading PDB identity from #Pdb stream (#69)

Add a new public API, md_get_pdb_id(), guarded by DNMD_PORTABLE_PDB,
that extracts the 20-byte PDB ID (GUID + stamp) from the #Pdb stream
of a portable PDB file. This enables consumers to validate that a PDB
file matches a PE assembly by comparing the PDB ID against the
CodeView debug directory entry in the PE.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Movement checks (#170)

* Replace wall collision penalty with proactive movement masking

- Add Room.can_link_move_from() for pixel-level movement checks using
  NES ObjGridOffset (tile checks only fire at grid boundaries)
- Add ZeldaGame.can_link_move() with locked-door-with-key override
- Add link_grid_offset to data.json (NES RAM 94)
- Remove WALL_COLLISION_PENALTY and _did_link_run_into_wall from critics
- Replace reactive _handle_wall_bump/move_mask in action_space with
  proactive per-direction can_link_move() calls in the mask builder
- Remove prevent_wall_bumping parameter from ZeldaActionSpace
- Replace stuck_count>=8 heuristic in frame_skip_wrapper with
  deterministic can_link_move_from() check at movement start
- Update tests: wall collision test → movement masking test,
  adjust stalfos injury timing for fewer wasted frames

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix movement masking: always check tiles regardless of grid_offset

ObjGridOffset is axis-agnostic — a nonzero offset from vertical movement
was incorrectly allowing horizontal moves into walls. The tile check is
conservative and never produces false positives, so always apply it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Always build Room from current RAM tiles to fix stale door corridors

ZeldaGame.room now always calls Room.create() with current_tiles instead
of preferring the cache via Room.get(). Room.create() short-circuits with
torch.equal() when tiles haven't changed, so the common case is cheap.

This fixes stale corridor tiles when doors open (key used or enemies
cleared) — the cached Room previously retained the locked-door tile
layout, causing movement masking to block valid corridor movement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Allow movement into cave entry tiles for proactive masking

Cave top tile (0xF3) is above OW_FIRST_UNWALKABLE but the NES triggers
a cave transition via CheckPassiveTileObjects (Z_07.asm:2957-2962) when
Link walks into it.  Treat it as walkable for movement masking so the
agent can enter caves.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restrict locked-door-with-key override to doorway corridor

The NES CheckDoorway (Z_05.asm:3755) only fires when Link's
perpendicular coordinate matches the doorway: X=$78 for N/S doors,
Y=$8D for E/W doors.  Without this position check, any room with a
locked door would allow movement toward the door from anywhere in the
room, even when blocked by walls/blocks (e.g. debug_1_45.state).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix empty wavefront when exit seed is blocked by obstacles

The wavefront used a single center exit tile offset off-screen as its
BFS seed.  If obstacles blocked expansion from that one point, the
wavefront never reached the rest of the room (e.g. debug_0_68.state).

Two fixes:
- Use ALL exit tiles as off-screen seeds so the BFS can expand from
  every exit point, not just the center.
- Skip can_move checks when expanding from off-screen seeds since they
  are virtual positions with no real NES tiles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update scenarios

* Fix pixel-alignment bug in can_link_move_from using exact NES hotspots

can_link_move_from was delegating to can_move which used approximate tile-
coordinate offsets (tr+1 for feet). The NES computes hotspot pixel positions
directly: base_Y = ObjY + $0B, then subtracts $40 for the status bar before
dividing by 8 to get the tile row.

The old formula (py - 56) // 8 + 1 disagreed with the NES formula
(py - 53) // 8 for 62% of Y positions (when (py-64) % 8 < 5), checking
one row too low. This caused false positives — allowing movement the NES
would block.

Rewrote can_link_move_from to compute exact NES hotspot positions:
- Left:  col=(px-8)//8,   row=(py-53)//8
- Right: col=(px+16)//8,  row=(py-53)//8
- Up:    col=px//8,        row=(py-61)//8,  checks col and col+1
- Down:  col=px//8,        row=(py-45)//8,  checks col and col+1

can_move (tile-space) left unchanged for wavefront BFS which is internally
consistent with its approximate offsets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- core: config/, dataclass_models/, exceptions/, interfaces/, logging/
- plugins: context/, examples/

Each new subdirectory has a mod.rs re-exporting its modules.

feat: Category and Status management pages (#29)

Implements admin-only Category and Status management pages for Issue #23.

## Backend (Domain layer)
- MediatR Commands for Categories: CreateCategoryCommand, UpdateCategoryCommand, ArchiveCategoryCommand
- MediatR Queries for Categories: GetCategoriesQuery, GetCategoryByIdQuery
- MediatR Commands for Statuses: CreateStatusCommand, UpdateStatusCommand, ArchiveStatusCommand
- MediatR Queries for Statuses: GetStatusesQuery, GetStatusByIdQuery
- FluentValidation validators for create/update commands

## Services (Web layer)
- CategoryService: Facade for category operations via MediatR
- StatusService: Facade for status operations via MediatR

## Admin Pages (Blazor)
- /admin - Updated dashboard with navigation cards
- /admin/categories - Full CRUD for categories with archive/restore
- /admin/statuses - Full CRUD for statuses with archive/restore
- AdminPageLayout.razor - Shared admin navigation component

## Features
- Create, edit, archive/restore categories and statuses
- Show archived toggle filter
- Success/error message feedback
- Modal forms with validation
- Dark mode support
- Tailwind CSS styling consistent with existing pages
- Admin-only authorization via AdminPolicy

Closes #23

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add v1.0 Phase 3: Docker Compose, config repo, goal input, multi-repo support

- Docker Compose deployment with orchestrator + 4 worker roles, health checks,
  scaling support, Swarm-ready. Orchestrator Dockerfile (multi-stage ASP.NET).
  Worker Dockerfile updated to include Worker .NET client alongside Copilot CLI.
- Config repo integration: YAML schema (HiveConfigFile), ConfigRepoManager for
  clone/pull, AGENTS.md read/commit, allowed-repos enforcement. YamlDotNet 16.3.0.
- Goal input system: IGoalSource interface, FileGoalSource (YAML), ApiGoalSource
  (REST endpoints /api/goals), GoalManager multi-source aggregator with priority.
- Multi-repo support: BranchCoordinator (copilothive/{goal}/{role}-{iter} naming),
  TaskBuilder for multi-repo TaskAssignment proto construction.
- 50 new tests (172 total): 17 config, 18 goals, 15 multi-repo.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(matter): resolve SBMD endpoints by device type matching (#174)

The original method used to locate the Matter device endpoint to use for
a resource originated from the method used by Zigbee. Zigbee doesn't
have the device types and parts list attributes which are much better
suited to locate endpoints hosting desired functionality. Our drivers
are for specific device types (on/off light, dimmable light, etc). This
maps directly to the device types attribute on Matter endpoints and is
more reliable than just looking for an endpoint with a target cluster as
before.

- Add endpoint map (sbmdEndpointMap) to MatterDevice, built during
AddDevice() by matching device type lists against driver's
matterMeta.deviceTypes
- Add ResolveEndpointMap() and GetEndpointForSbmdIndex() methods
- Update BindResourceReadInfo, BindWriteInfo, BindExecuteInfo, and
BindResourceEventInfo to accept optional sbmdEndpointIndex parameter
- Pre-resolve Matter endpoint IDs at bind time; device-level resources
fall back to GetEndpointForCluster()
- Store resolvedEndpointId on write/execute bindings for use at
invocation time
- Update SpecBasedMatterDeviceDriver to pass endpoint index for
endpoint-level resources and std::nullopt for device-level resources
- Add MatterDeviceEndpointMapTest with 27 tests covering endpoint map
resolution, index lookup, and all four binding methods
- Add device-type-endpoint-resolution spec to openspec/specs
- Archive endpoints-from-device-types change

Refs: BARTON-358

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add CopilotHive.Worker — C# client service for worker containers

Implements the worker-side service that runs inside Docker containers:

- Program.cs: Entry point parsing env vars (ORCHESTRATOR_URL, WORKER_ROLE,
  WORKER_ID, WORKER_CAPABILITIES, COPILOT_PORT) with graceful SIGTERM handling
- WorkerService.cs: Core lifecycle — gRPC registration, heartbeat (30s),
  bidirectional WorkStream, task dispatch, AGENTS.md updates
- GitOperations.cs: Git CLI operations (clone, checkout, branch, push, status)
  with ForceDeleteDirectoryAsync for Windows file-lock safety
- CopilotRunner.cs: JSON-RPC HTTP client for headless Copilot CLI communication
- TaskExecutor.cs: Full task lifecycle — clone repos, handle branches, send
  prompt to Copilot, collect git status, report completion

References CopilotHive.Shared for gRPC proto types. All 122 existing tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: CopilotHive
repository.description: Self-improving multi-agent orchestration system powered by GitHub Copilot

commit.message:
Fix security vulnerabilities in auth endpoints (closes #13) (#15)

Security Fixes:
- Add open redirect prevention: Validate returnUrl is local path before redirecting
- Change logout from GET to POST: Prevents CSRF logout attacks
- Update LoginDisplay.razor: Use form with POST method and AntiforgeryToken

Implementation Details:
- Added IsLocalUrl() helper to validate URLs start with / but not //
- Rejects http://, https://, and protocol-relative URLs
- Logout endpoint now requires POST method
- Antiforgery validation via UseAntiforgery() middleware

Testing:
- Added Web.Tests project with integration tests
- Tests verify URL validation logic for open redirect protection
- Tests verify GET logout returns 404 (endpoint removed)
- Tests verify POST logout requires authentication
- All 12 tests passing

References:
- OWASP Open Redirect: https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Unvalidated_Redirects_and_Forwards_Cheat_Sheet.html

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add gRPC server layer to orchestrator

Add ASP.NET gRPC service on top of the existing orchestrator,
enabling distributed mode via --serve flag while preserving
the legacy CLI mode.

New files:
- Services/HiveOrchestratorService.cs — gRPC service implementing
  Register, WorkStream (bidirectional), and Heartbeat RPCs
- Services/WorkerPool.cs — thread-safe connected worker management
- Services/TaskQueue.cs — role-aware task queue with active tracking
- Services/ConnectedWorker.cs — worker state with per-worker Channel

Changes:
- CopilotHive.csproj: SDK → Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web, added Grpc.AspNetCore
- Program.cs: dual mode (--serve for gRPC server, default for CLI)
- /health endpoint for Docker health checks

Implement agentic multi-tool AI system with file attachments

- chaosgpt-zeta.html: Add agentic CSS (agent panel, download card, attachment
  area, EXIF table, entropy meter, file type badge styles)
- chaosgpt-zeta.html: New footer with attachment area div, paperclip button,
  and hidden file input supporting image/audio/video/code/binary files
- chaosgpt-zeta.html: Add attachedFiles state variable; modify sendMessage to
  include attachments + agentic flag; clear attachments after send
- chaosgpt-zeta.html: Hook into streaming path to auto-dispatch tool_call
  blocks parsed from AI responses via processAgentResponse()
- chaosgpt-zeta.html: Full agentic tool engine script block with:
  - File attachment state management (handleFileSelect, renderAttachmentChips)
  - Tool dispatcher (stego_embed, stego_extract, exif_read, exif_write,
    file_analyze, code_generate, python_run)
  - Agent reasoning panel UI with step-by-step status display
  - LSB image steganography encode/decode
  - MP3 append-after-EOF steganography
  - MP4 free-atom steganography
  - JPEG EXIF parser and stripper
  - File magic-byte detection, Shannon entropy, hex dump, stego detection
  - C# code generation helper
  - Pyodide Python runner (lazy-loaded)
  - Download card rendering for binary outputs
  - Agent follow-up via non-streaming chat API
- chaosgpt-zeta-backend.js: Complete Cloudflare Worker with:
  - JWT sign/verify (HMAC-SHA256)
  - Auth: login, signup, check-username, reset-password
  - Models endpoint (Mistral, Gemini, xAI tiers)
  - Chat handler with streaming (SSE), attachment context, tool results,
    agentic system prompt injection, web search integration
  - Conversation history CRUD
  - Title generation endpoint

commit.message:
Add workflow flexibility and orchestrator protocol tests (122 tests)

- Brain PlanIterationAsync returning Done now short-circuits the iteration
- Carry forward previous metrics when brain skips an iteration
- Add PlanIterationOverride to FakeOrchestratorBrain for testability
- Add OrchestratorProtocolTests: JSON serialization, ParseFromLlmResponse,
  markdown code block extraction, snake_case naming, default values
- Add workflow flexibility integration tests: Done skips workers,
  metrics carry-forward, plan override receives correct arguments

commit.message:
Task 3.5: add golangci-lint config and fix all lint issues (#9)

* feat: add golangci-lint config and fix all lint issues (Task 3.5)

- .golangci.yml: enable errcheck, govet, staticcheck (linters) and
  gofmt, goimports (formatters); exclude internal/gen from all checks
- server.go: remove redundant http.Handler type annotation on wrapped
  (flagged by staticcheck ST1023)
- server_test.go: replace bare defer resp.Body.Close() with
  defer func() { _ = resp.Body.Close() }() (flagged by errcheck)
- static.go: apply gofmt alignment fix to struct literal fields
